home.title: Home
home.newUserDescription: Complete the following two steps, and your home page will generate today's action, weekly progress, and body status analysis.
home.newUserStep1: Record your first run (3km or more)
home.newUserStep2: Complete personal profile (max heart rate, weekly mileage)
home.bodyStatusDescription:
Click the card to see detailed status explanation

Fatigue: Recent training load accumulation (ATL)
Fitness: Long-term aerobic capacity (CTL)
Form: Balance of fitness-fatigue (TSB)
home.emptyTodayAction: Record at least one run over 3km before the system can generate today's action
home.emptyWeeklyProgress: After saving your personal profile, this will show your weekly goal progress

weeklyProgress.remainingDistance: {{distance}} km remaining this week, {{intensity}} intensity workouts and long run training still needed
weeklyProgress.viewWeeklyRecords: Click here to view weekly training records and progress
weeklyProgress.intensityWorkoutDefinition: Intensity workouts are defined as training at or above lactate threshold
weeklyProgress.longRunRequirements:
Full Marathon preparation standards

To finish safely without hitting the wall, pre-race long runs should meet:

• Minimum effective long run: 25km
• Recommended target long run: 28-32km
• Maximum preparation long run: 35-36km
  (Not recommended to exceed 36km)
weeklyProgress.weeklyTargetAchieved: Weekly mileage target achieved. Tap to see if you need to maintain the pace.
weeklyProgress.weeklyTargetAlmostDone: Very close to weekly target. Tap to see remaining training arrangements.
weeklyProgress.weeklyProgressDefault: Tap to view weekly training records and progress
weeklyProgress.intensityZoneHeader: Intensity training pace and heart rate zones based on current VDOT:

tools.timeRangeMessage: Target time should be between 2–8 hours
tools.hydrationTip: Take small sips at every water station

profile.title: Personal Profile

errors.maxHrRange: Max heart rate should be between 150–220
errors.restingHrRange: Resting heart rate should be between 30–80
errors.thresholdHrRange: Threshold heart rate should be between 130–200
errors.weeklyKmRange: Weekly distance should be between 5–250 km
errors.birthYearRequired: Please enter a valid birth year first

profile.estimateMaxHr: Estimate max heart rate by age
profile.estimateMaxHrMessage:
Based on age ({{age}} years), estimated max heart rate is {{hr}}.
Recommend replacing with actual measured value later.

hints.maxHrAccuracy: Most accurate with measured value (e.g., max HR after sprint)
hints.restingHrMeasure: Measure upon waking in the morning
hints.weeklyKmUsage: Baseline reference for training plans and prescriptions

profile.trainingToolsDescription: Pre-race planning and training arrangement tools, use as needed.
profile.trainingPlanDescription: Generate race cycle training arrangement, suitable for season planning.
profile.raceAssistantDescription: Set target finish time, view pre-race and race day strategies.
profile.backupDescription: All data is stored locally only. Please export backup before deleting the app, reinstalling, or changing devices.
profile.backupSuccessMessage: Data exported. Please save to a secure location (such as iCloud Drive).
profile.subtitle: Personalized training analysis based on your data
profile.importResult:
Successfully imported {{imported}} records
Skipped {{skipped}} duplicate records

input.title: Running Record
input.ocrDescription: Local recognition only, supports Keep, Garmin, Apple Watch and other common screenshots

alerts.confirmImport:
Import all {{count}} running records?

This may take some time.
alerts.localRecognitionComplete: Local recognition complete, confidence {{confidence}}%, please confirm data before submitting

input.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this running record?
input.cameraPermissionPrompt: Please allow camera access in Settings
input.testReminderMessage: You will receive a local notification in 5 seconds to confirm the reminder function works.
input.needRunningRecord: Please first record at least one running session over 3km to calculate VDOT
input.needMinWeeks: At least 2 weeks needed to generate training plan
input.noHealthData: No running records found. Please ensure there is running data in Apple Health
input.firstSyncNote: First sync may take a while, please wait

history.title: History
history.currentWeekFocusText: The top group shows this week's training. Quickly check weekly goal completion.

analysis.bodyRecovering: Body is still recovering, use very low intensity easy running to promote blood circulation.
analysis.currentRecoveryHigh: Current recovery stress is high, prioritize rest today or only very light recovery activities.
analysis.thisWasEasyRun: This was easy run, recovery is good.
analysis.thisWeekRemainingDistance: After this training, you still need {{distance}} km to reach this week's goal.
analysis.restAfterHighDays: After consecutive high-intensity training, full rest is required for recovery.
analysis.restHighFatigue: Fatigue index is high (TSB < {{threshold}}), complete rest or very light walking recommended.
analysis.longRunDesc: This week's long run, maintain easy pace to build aerobic endurance.
analysis.intervalDesc:
Good form, interval training to boost VO2max.
Suggested: 1km×5 sets, jog 400m between sets.
analysis.tempoDesc:
Lactate threshold training to improve sustained pace.
Suggested: 20-30 min continuous run after warmup.
analysis.marathonTempoDesc:
Marathon race pace specific training.
Suggested: run at M pace for 30-40 min after warmup.
analysis.easyRunDesc: Easy aerobic run, maintain mileage without adding fatigue. Can converse easily.
analysis.easyRunDesc2: Easy run at a comfortable pace, enjoy running.
analysis.activeRecoveryDesc: Active recovery day, complete rest or walking, yoga and other cross training.
analysis.bodyStatusInfoDetail:
TSB > 0: Fitness > Fatigue → Good form, suitable for racing
  Race targets: +10 to +25 (after 10-14 days taper)

TSB ≈ 0: Fitness ≈ Fatigue → Adapting to training

TSB < 0: Fatigue > Fitness → Accumulated fatigue, injury risk
analysis.peakTip: Recovery is excellent, ideal for racing or a high-quality training session.
analysis.normalTip: Load is in control, maintain training pace.
analysis.readySubtitle: Body is in good shape, follow today's training plan.
analysis.normalSubtitle: Body is stable, follow today's training plan.
analysis.tiredSubtitle: Some fatigue accumulated, focus on controlling intensity today.
analysis.restSubtitle: Current recovery stress is high, prioritize rest today or only very light recovery activities.
analysis.conservativeStartNote: Conservative start, 10–15 sec/km slower than target, avoid overextending
analysis.conservativeFinishNote: Conservative maintenance, prioritize finishing, don't force pace
analysis.gel3Note: 3rd energy gel, most critical fueling point
analysis.preRacePlan10_7: 6–8km easy run daily, maintain fitness, don't increase load
analysis.preRacePlan6_3: Under 5km slow jog daily, focus on recovery
analysis.preRacePlanRace: Eat 2 hours before start, execute pacing strategy
analysis.recoveryPlanSubtitle1: Fatigue index is high, recommended 1-week recovery plan below
analysis.recoveryPlanSubtitle2: Severe fatigue accumulation, recommended 2-week recovery plan below
analysis.suggestLongRun: Prioritize recovery after long run, rest or easy jog.
analysis.riskHighHR: Heart rate was high, reduce load.
analysis.riskConsecutive: Consecutive high-intensity runs, injury risk rising.

trainingPlan.phaseBaseDesc: Build aerobic base, mostly easy runs, gradually increase mileage
trainingPlan.phaseBuildDesc: Introduce intensity (tempo/interval), improve lactate threshold and VO2max
trainingPlan.phasePeakDesc: Increase high-intensity ratio, simulate race pace
trainingPlan.phaseTaperDesc: Reduce volume, maintain intensity, peak for competition
trainingPlan.focusBase2: Steadily increase mileage, build aerobic base
trainingPlan.focusBase3: End of base phase, prepare for quality training
trainingPlan.focusBuildRecovery: Recovery week: reduce mileage, absorb fatigue
trainingPlan.focusTaperFinal: Final week, fully recover, ready to race!
trainingPlan.longRunNote: Complete long run at easy pace
trainingPlan.longRunMNote: Long run, last 3km try M pace
trainingPlan.intervalNote: Warmup 2km → 1km×5 sets (I-pace) → 400m jog between sets → Cooldown 2km

retention.goalCompletedMsg: This training completed {{weekLabel}} mileage goal, focus on pace and recovery.
retention.longRunCompletedMsg: This training covered the key long run for {{weekLabel}}.
retention.qualityMetMsg: This training met {{weekLabel}} intensity workout goal.
retention.qualityProgressMsg: This training is building towards {{weekLabel}} intensity workout goal.
retention.remainingMsg: After this training, {{km}} km remaining to reach {{weekLabel}} mileage goal.
retention.maintainMsg: This training helps maintain {{weekLabel}} training rhythm.

reminder.notificationBody: Open RunForge to see today's training.

vdotProgression.meaning_beginner: Capable of completing a half/full marathon, focusing on health runs and easy pacing
vdotProgression.meaning_intermediate: Consistent long-distance ability, can compete for PB at mass events
vdotProgression.meaning_eliteAmateur: Top of the pack at events, capable of systematic intervals and threshold training
vdotProgression.meaning_amateurElite: Near professional level, can compete for national event rankings
vdotProgression.meaning_professional: Top endurance level, representing the limits of long-distance running
vdotProgression.beginnerStage: You are in the beginner accumulation stage
vdotProgression.eliteStage: You have reached the top endurance stage
